behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels for attendee attribution
behaviors.justification: A pure Unit mock always returns the expected format (e.g., `{speaker: 'Alice', text: '...'}`) and hides real API format mismatches (e.g., API returns `{speaker_id: 1, ...}` or drops speaker info entirely). The service's label-handling logic must interact with realistic transcription API output to catch format/parsing bugs. Integration testing with test audio + real (or realistic) API response is the minimum to detect this.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real or test-instrumented speech-to-text API with sample audio containing multiple speakers, capture the raw response, verify the service correctly extracts and preserves speaker identity (name or ID) through to the summary output. Test with edge cases: silent attendees, overlapping speech, speaker rejoining.

behaviors.description: Structured summary contains at least one action item per attendee assigned a task
behaviors.justification: A mock LLM always returns structurally correct output with all required fields present. Real LLM failure modes: misses implicit task assignments ('Alice, can you follow up?'), attributes tasks to the wrong person, drops attendees entirely, or returns garbled JSON. The semantic judgment of what constitutes a task assignment and whether the LLM correctly extracted it is non-deterministic and depends on real model behavior. No mock or deterministic logic can substitute this.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real LLM with transcripts from recorded meetings where N attendees were explicitly assigned tasks. Verify the summary's action_items array includes at least one item per assignee, with correct owner attribution. Test with implicit assignments (hints, questions directed at people) and ambiguous language.

behaviors.description: Key decisions appear in 'decisions' section of summary, not buried in narrative
behaviors.justification: A mock LLM always produces the expected structure with a populated decisions field. Real failure: the LLM conflates decisions with action items, misses implicit decisions ('We've decided to pause this feature'), outputs decisions in the narrative field instead, or produces empty decisions array when the transcript contains clear decisions. The semantic distinction between decision/action/narrative and LLM's ability to place content in the correct output field are LLM-dependent and non-deterministic.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real LLM with meeting transcripts containing explicit and implicit decisions mixed with other content (action items, context). Verify the summary.decisions array is populated with substantive decisions only, separate from action_items and narrative. Validate output schema conformance and section boundaries.

behaviors.description: Audio >60 minutes is split into segments, processed sequentially, and merged into coherent summary
behaviors.justification: The orchestration (split, sequence, merge) can be unit-tested with mocks of the transcription API. But the real failure boundary is whether the merged transcript remains semantically coherent when the LLM re-processes it: Does the LLM lose cross-segment context? Does it repeat decisions/items from different segments? Does the summary read as one document or fragmented? This coherence judgment is non-deterministic and LLM-dependent. A mock LLM would always pass, hiding context-loss bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Upload a real meeting recording >60 minutes (or synthesized test audio of that length), run the full pipeline with segment splitting enabled. Verify that the final summary is coherent (no repetition, cross-segment context preserved, attendee mentions consistent across segments). Manually review or use a second LLM to score coherence. Compare against single-pass transcription baseline.

behaviors.description: Full pipeline (upload, transcribe, summarize, deliver) completes without manual intervention on real meeting recording
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a multi-step pipeline with two external agentic dependencies: (1) real speech-to-text API call with real audio upload (System), (2) real LLM call to summarize (Agentic), both in sequence. A mock at any level hides integration failures: timeouts, partial results, exception handling, state loss between steps, delivery failures. Unit or Integration tests with mocks would report success while the real pipeline hangs or crashes. The minimum level to catch end-to-end failure is Workflow: multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real meeting recording (10–30 min, diverse speakers, clear audio) to the service and monitor the full pipeline from upload through delivery. Verify no manual intervention is needed: file uploads successfully, transcription completes, LLM summarization runs, structured output is delivered in expected format and accessible via API/webhook. Verify intermediate states (transcribing, summarizing, complete) and error handling (graceful failure or retry on API timeout).
